name: West Nile
story: Home to some of the country's largest refugee-hosting settlements, sharing schools, clinics and farmland with 1.8 million people displaced from neighbouring conflicts.

name: Karamoja
story: New roads and boreholes are reaching pastoral communities once cut off for months at a time during the rains.

name: Acholi (Northern)
story: Post-conflict recovery rebuilt schools and workshops; a new power line now keeps small businesses running through the day.

name: Busoga & Elgon (Eastern)
story: The Source of the Nile Bridge at Jinja and the coffee terraces of Mount Elgon anchor the region's trade routes.

name: Central (Kampala)
story: The Kampala–Entebbe Expressway and the national grid's hub sit at the country's economic centre.

name: Rwenzori & Ankole (Western)
story: Hydropower from the Nile basin extends the grid west, powering dairy, coffee, and a growing university town at Mbarara.
